label: ∅ | live | man
sentence: In a fertile land of ancient Persia, there lived a humble man with three sons.

label: young | ridicule | ∅
sentence: The youngest, called Rustam the Simple, was ridiculed and scorned by all, a gentle soul seen as foolish.

label: son | plan | ∅
sentence: One day, the eldest son planned to enter the vast Zagros forest to gather fragrant cedar wood.

label: mother | give | cake
sentence: Before he set out, his mother gave him a sweet honey cake laced with rosewater and a flask of pomegranate wine, to stave off hunger and thirst.

label: he | encounter | small
sentence: As he journeyed beneath the ancient cypress trees, he encountered a small, wizened dervish with a snowy beard.

label: dervish | greet | he
sentence: The dervish greeted him warmly and pleaded, “Share but a morsel of your cake and a sip of your wine; my hunger and thirst weigh heavy.”

label: son | reply | ∅
sentence: But the eldest son, proud and shrewd, replied, “If I share my cake and wine, I shall have none for myself.

label: he | stride | ∅
sentence: and he strode on, leaving the poor man behind.

label: axe | slip | ∅
sentence: When he tried to chop a towering cedar, his axe slipped, and he wounded his arm deeply.

label: pride | wound | ∅
sentence: He had no choice but to return home, his pride wounded as well.

label: misfortune | be | retribution
sentence: This misfortune was the dervish’s subtle retribution.

label: son | venture | ∅
sentence: The second son next ventured out, his mother again sending him off with a fragrant saffron cake and a jar of mulberry wine.

label: dervish | appear | ∅
sentence: The same dervish appeared and asked for a share, but the second son cunningly said, “If I give away my food and drink, I’ll be left empty.

label: he | go | ∅
sentence: Ignoring the plea, he went on, only to soon strike his own leg with the axe, forcing him home in pain.

label: rustam | beg | ∅
sentence: Finally, Rustam the Simple begged earnestly, “Father, let me go and gather wood too.”

label: work | be | you
sentence: The father hesitated, “Your brothers were harmed; such work is not for you.”

label: plea | cease | ∅
sentence: But Rustam’s pleas would not cease, and at last his father relented, “Go then, and learn by your own trials.”

label: mother | give | cake
sentence: His mother, with less hope, gave him a humble cake baked in ashes and a bottle of vinegary sour drink from fermented barley.

label: dervish | find | rustam
sentence: In the forest, the old dervish found Rustam, greeting him with a humble smile and repeating his request for cake and wine.

label: ∅ | let | ∅
sentence: Rustam replied kindly, “All I have is this ash-baked cake and sour beer; if you are content with them, let us share.”

label: they | sit | tree
sentence: They sat beneath a fragrant jasmine tree, and as Rustam offered his simple fare, it transformed— the ash cake becoming a sweetened delicacy rich with honey and almonds, the sour beer turning to fine Shiraz wine.

label: they | feast | ∅
sentence: They feasted together, and the dervish said, “Because your heart is generous, I bless you with fortune.

label: ∅ | stand | cypress
sentence: There stands an ancient cypress—fell it, and you will find a treasure at its roots.”

label: he | vanish | ∅
sentence: Then he vanished as mysteriously as he came.

label: rustam | cut | tree
sentence: Rustam cut down the tree, and beneath it nestled a magnificent goose with feathers of gleaming gold, shimmering like the sun over the desert dunes.

label: he | lift | bird
sentence: He lifted the golden bird and journeyed to an inn to rest.

label: daughter | long | ∅
sentence: The innkeeper’s three daughters, curious about the wondrous bird, longed to possess a golden feather.

label: eld | creep | ∅
sentence: The eldest crept close and grasped a feather, but instantly her hand clung fast to the goose’s wing.

label: sister | follow | ∅
sentence: Her sister soon followed, grasping her sibling, and found herself stuck as well.

label: ∅ | approach | ∅
sentence: The youngest, undeterred by their cries, approached and touched her sisters, becoming bound to them.

label: they | force | ∅
sentence: So, they were forced to spend the night tethered to Rustam’s golden goose.

label: rustam | set | ∅
sentence: The next dawn, Rustam set off, ignoring the weighty procession trailing behind him.

label: mullah | encounter | train
sentence: Along their path, a mullah encountered the curious train.

label: he | scold | ∅
sentence: “What folly is this—young women chasing a man through the fields?” he scolded and seized the youngest daughter’s hand, but he too became bound and was pulled along.

label: assistant | come | scene
sentence: Soon after, the mullah’s assistant came across the scene and was drawn into the odd procession.

label: ∅ | compel | ∅
sentence: Then two farmers, working their fields with curved sickles, were compelled to join, and now seven figures followed Rustam, laughing at their own predicament.

label: she | laugh | ∅
sentence: At last, they arrived at the court of a powerful Shah whose daughter was veiled not only in splendor but sorrow— she never laughed, and no joy could break her stern exterior.

label: shah | declare | ∅
sentence: The Shah declared that whoever could make his daughter laugh would win her hand.

label: she | burst | laughter
sentence: When the princess glimpsed the curious sight of the chained group behind Rustam, she burst into unexpected laughter, the sound like silver bells across the palace gardens.

label: rustam | ask | hand
sentence: Rustam asked for her hand, but the Shah, displeased with the simple youth, demanded impossible trials first.

label: task | be | ∅
sentence: His first task was to find a man who could drink an entire cellar of wine.

label: rustam | return | forest
sentence: Rustam returned to the forest where the dervish had sat, now a sorrowful figure thirsting beyond all relief, having emptied vast barrels yet still parched.

label: rustam | say | ∅
sentence: “Come with me,” said Rustam, “and drink to your heart’s content.”

label: thirst | quench | ∅
sentence: At the Shah’s cellar, the man drank deeply until every barrel was empty, his thirst finally quenched.

label: shah | declare | ∅
sentence: The Shah then declared Rustam must find a man who could consume a mountain of bread.

label: rustam | venture | ∅
sentence: Rustam ventured again and found a gaunt giant tying his belt tighter about his waist, declaring his hunger was endless despite feasting on a full oven of baked flatbreads.

label: rustam | say | ∅
sentence: “Come, you shall fill your hunger,” said Rustam.

label: mound | present | palace
sentence: At the palace an enormous mound of freshly baked bread was presented, and the giant devoured it all by day’s end.

label: shah | demand | ∅
sentence: Now the Shah demanded that Rustam produce a ship that could travel both on land and on water.

label: rustam | find | dervish
sentence: Guided once more to the forest, Rustam found the dervish who rewarded his kindness by gifting him a wondrous vessel, shaped like a swift gazelle and enchanted to glide across both earth and river.

label: shah | deny | rustam
sentence: Seeing this marvel, the Shah could no longer deny Rustam his daughter’s hand.

label: wedding | celebrate | ∅
sentence: A grand wedding was celebrated, and after the Shah’s passing, Rustam inherited the kingdom, living many prosperous years with his joyful bride.

label: simpleton | honor | ∅
sentence: Thus, the humble and kind-hearted simpleton was honored, his golden goose a symbol of fortune born from generosity and grace.
